ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for pineapple bun with custard filling; 'bun food product' (FOODON_00002683) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Sweet buns and rolls) is the closest parent; no specific code exists for custard-filled pineapple bun.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for this specific bakery product.

main_ingredients: custard filling (egg yolk, sugar, milk, cornstarch, butter)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard pineapple bun with molten custard filling, approximately 100 g, as typically served at a Hong Kong-style bing sutt or Singapore kopitiam.

nutrient_source.title: Pineapple Bun (Bo Lo Bao) — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Branded Foods composite; adapted with custard-filling adjustment using USDA custard data
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for custard-filled pineapple bun exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Values are estimated by combining USDA SR Legacy data for sweet enriched bread/bun (NDB 18029, ~300 kcal/100g base) with USDA custard filling data (NDB 19095), weighted approximately 75% bun shell and 25% custard filling by mass, consistent with typical Hong Kong-style polo bao construction. This is a medium-confidence composite estimate.

gi_source.title: White bread and enriched sweet bun GI reference — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for custard-filled pineapple bun
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for custard-filled pineapple bun (polo bao). GI estimated at 72 based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white enriched sweet bread/bun (GI ~70–75). The custard filling contributes additional sugar but the overall GI is dominated by the refined wheat flour shell. GL per serving = (72 × 50.5 g carbohydrate × 1.0 serving of 100 g) / 100 = 36.4.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g) and refined carbohydrate load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should limit portion size to half a bun and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods, as the moderate sodium content combined with a high refined-carbohydrate load can contribute to fluid retention and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (72) and high sugar content (~18 g/100 g) from refined flour shell and custard filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this bun as an occasional indulgence only; the high GI of 72 and approximately 18 g of sugar per 100 g will cause a rapid postprandial blood glucose spike, and portion should be restricted to half a bun with a low-GI meal to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~6.8 g/100 g), cholesterol (~95 mg/100 g), and trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g) from butter and egg-yolk custard
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this bun due to its saturated fat and cholesterol content from butter and egg-yolk custard; occasional consumption of one small bun is acceptable within a heart-healthy dietary pattern that otherwise limits saturated fat to less than 7% of total energy.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 发财爆浆菠萝包 (Fā Cái Bào Jiāng Bōluó Bāo), known in English as the Fa Cai Molten Custard Pineapple Bun, is a festive variant of the classic Hong Kong polo bao (pineapple bun). The name '发财' (fā cái) means 'prosperity' or 'get rich', making it a popular choice during Chinese New Year and auspicious occasions. The bun features the signature crumbly, golden, sugar-cookie-style crust that gives the pineapple bun its name — despite containing no actual pineapple — and is filled with a rich, molten egg-custard centre that flows when the bun is broken open. The shell is made from enriched bread dough with butter, sugar, eggs, and milk, while the custard filling is prepared from egg yolks, sugar, milk, cornstarch, and butter, then chilled before being encased in the dough and baked. The result is a warm, soft bun with a crisp, sweet topping and a luscious, creamy interior. Nutritionally, the bun is energy-dense at approximately 340 kcal per 100 g, with a high carbohydrate content (~50.5 g/100 g), moderate fat (~12.5 g/100 g, of which ~6.8 g is saturated), and moderate protein (~7.2 g/100 g). The high glycemic index (~72) and sugar content (~18 g/100 g) make it a high-concern food for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The saturated fat and cholesterol from butter and egg yolk are relevant for cardiovascular health. In Singapore, this bun is served at Hong Kong-style bing sutt (ice rooms) and kopitiams, and is typically enjoyed as a breakfast item or afternoon snack, often paired with kopi (local coffee) or milk tea. It is vegetarian and generally halal-certified when prepared without lard.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for custard-filled pineapple bun (polo bao with molten custard) was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy sweet enriched bun and custard filling data. GI is estimated by analogy from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white enriched sweet bread. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are composite estimates (75% bun shell, 25% custard filling by mass) derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for sweet enriched bread (NDB 18029) and egg custard (NDB 19095). No exact match was found in HPB Singapore Foodo. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 3. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy source entries used for this composite estimate. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on the University of Sydney GI Database entry for white enriched sweet bun/bread; no direct GI measurement for polo bao with custard filling was found. 6. The food name '发财' (fa cai / prosperity) is a marketing/festive prefix used by the restaurant; the base food is a custard-filled pineapple bun (polo bao). 7. Halal status is assumed based on typical preparation at Singapore kopitiams; lard-free preparation should be confirmed with the specific restaurant.
